steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: Text 2 explicitly calls the characterization in Text 1 “an overgeneralization” and then provides two historical counterexamples.

steps.title: Match to the answer
steps.content:
The examples of Barrett Browning (who published under her own name) and Willis (who chose a female pseudonym) demonstrate that Text 1's broad claim about women needing male pseudonyms is too sweeping — exactly what A identifies: “historical examples that challenge the assumption” in Text 1.

The other options misread the relationship —
  • B suggests Text 2 agrees with Text 1
  • C attributes unsupported claims about motivation
  • D is incomplete
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
